title: Chromesthesia: The Sound of Color
description: An immersive six-day synesthesia-inspired audio-visual exhibition blending projection mapping and spatial sound.
location: The Glasshouse Gallery, San Francisco, CA

body.raw:
# Chromesthesia: The Sound of Color

**A Multi-Sensory Journey into Synesthesia**

*   **Opening Date:** Thursday, January 15th, 2026
*   **Closing Date:** Tuesday, January 20th, 2026
*   **Location:** The Glasshouse Gallery, 404 Industrial Way, San Francisco, CA
*   **Admission:** Ticket Required (See below)

---

## 🎨 Event Overview

**Chromesthesia** is an immersive six-day exhibition challenging the boundaries of human perception. By merging high-fidelity audio engineering with large-scale projection mapping, this event simulates the neurological condition of *synesthesia*—where sound is seen, and color is heard.

Visitors will navigate through four distinct "zones," each calibrated to a specific audio frequency and color spectrum.

### The Zones
1.  **The Sub-Bass Blue Room:** Feel the color blue through 40Hz sub-woofers and liquid nitrogen fog.
2.  **The High-Frequency Yellow Lounge:** A sharp, lucid space utilizing violin concertos and stroboscopic lasers.
3.  **The Red Pulse:** An interactive heartbeat sensor that changes the room's hue based on the collective heart rate of the attendees.
4.  **The Silence:** A complete anechoic chamber where the only visual is the "noise" of your own optic nerve.

---

## 📅 Schedule of Events

| Date | Time | Activity | Location |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Jan 15** | 18:00 - 20:00 | **Grand Opening Gala** (VIP Only) | Main Hall |
| **Jan 15** | 20:00 - 23:00 | General Admission Entry | All Zones |
| **Jan 16** | 14:00 - 15:30 | *Talk: The Neuroscience of Art* | Lecture Hall B |
| **Jan 17** | 19:00 - 21:00 | Live Synth Performance by *Ocular Noise* | Blue Room |
| **Jan 18** | 10:00 - 14:00 | Family Interactive Workshop | Courtyard |
| **Jan 20** | 20:00 - 22:00 | **Closing Ceremony & Light-out** | Main Hall |

> *Note: The gallery is open for self-guided tours daily from 10:00 AM to 10:00 PM outside of specific event times.*

---

## 👥 Featured Artists

*   **Elena Vosc (Berlin)** – *Digital Projection Specialist*
    *   Known for her work on the Berlin Wall light commemoration.
*   **Marcus Thorne (London)** – *Acoustic Architect*
    *   Creator of the "Sound Sculptures" series.
*   **The Hive Mind (Collective)** – *Generative AI*
    *   An AI algorithm that paints in real-time based on ambient crowd noise.

---

## 🎟️ Ticket Information

Tickets are available online or at the door (subject to availability).

*   **General Admission:** $25.00
    *   *Includes access to all 4 zones.*
*   **Student / Senior:** $18.00
    *   *Requires valid ID.*
*   **VIP Opening Night Pass:** $75.00
    *   *Includes open bar, artist meet-and-greet, and early access.*
*   **Children (Under 12):** Free

[**Click Here to Purchase Tickets**](#) *(Link to checkout)*

---

## 📍 Venue & Accessibility

**The Glasshouse Gallery**
404 Industrial Way, District 9
San Francisco, CA 94107

*   **Parking:** Valet parking available on opening night. Public garage located at 3rd St.
*   **Public Transit:** Take the T-Third Line to Mariposa Street Station.
*   **Accessibility:** The venue is fully wheelchair accessible. Sensory-friendly hours are available on Sunday morning (Jan 18) with reduced volume and strobe effects disabled.

---

### Contact

For press inquiries or private tour bookings, please contact:
**Email:** hello@chromesthesia-event.com
**Phone:** +1 (415) 555-0199
